notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Inspected get_case and list_evidence: four juror-visible system case-packet text files only, no submitted evidence/exhibits/arguments. Read full ranges and stats for all four. Market page ev_2c7c2c8a2d76 identifies Kalshi title, PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION family, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes. Pre-event reporter exchange ev_9cd6fa5297f3 is Dec. 5, 2025 transcript at JFK Center before FIFA World Cup Draw; it contains Trump saying standalone "Ronaldo" in soccer-related answer; note says released Dec. 8, corrected Jan. 2, incomplete audio portions. Terms ev_a373371a8c9c define word/time period/event rules; event time period can include event contexts and official/published content, excludes behind-scenes/unofficial unless officially broadcast/published. Formal archive note ev_eac0f81b7818 says a separate formal attendance transcript page exists on Roll Call/Factba.se but full text not retrieved due password protection; it does not establish whether formal program includes Ronaldo. Native web adverse/source-map check: govinfo DCPD-202501162 appears to mirror the official transcript; UCSB URL from packet is source URL; Dailymotion page has transcript snippets; Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript remains adverse/limiting target. Because opening phase does not allow submit_evidence, no outside source is relied on as record proof. Theory: word itself is not seriously disputed on packet; dispute is boundary of "during the FIFA World Cup Draw." Plaintiff argues market/terms did not clearly exclude public event-linked pre-program remarks at venue immediately before formal program. Need later admit official transcript/source captures and pursue formal-transcript adverse check when evidence admission opens. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes, 2026-06-29. Objective: record opening statement for defendant in arb-ex03-20260629192557. Case theory: plaintiff can point to a case-packet transcript containing the standalone word Ronaldo, but the decisive issue is whether that pre-program reporter exchange is within the market's time/event boundary, "during the FIFA World Cup Draw." Record scanned via get_case/list_evidence/read_evidence_range. Evidence list has four system case-packet files and no submitted evidence. Market page: title "What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?"; family PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION; strike Ronaldo; challenged resolution No; requested Yes. Terms: underlying is words used by entity during time period; time period can be event/context; for a specific event Exchange may specify what is included; behind-the-scenes/rehearsals/sound checks/unofficial recordings excluded unless officially broadcast/published as part of the time period; words must be distinct standalone units. Pre-event reporter exchange: title says "Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw"; Dec. 5, 2025; joined in progress; President at Kennedy Center; source URL UCSB Presidency Project; note says released Dec. 8 and corrected Jan. 2, with portions not verifiable because audio incomplete. It contains Ronaldo in soccer discussion. Formal-draw archive note: separate Roll Call/Factba.se archive page exists for Trump's formal attendance remarks at the draw, but full transcript was not retrieved due password protection. It does not establish formal program did or did not contain Ronaldo, but it supports a distinction between pre-event reporter exchange and formal draw remarks. Outside investigation: web searches for exact Presidency Project phrase and market phrase found AgentCourt examples and a Substack dispute summary. Substack, not admitted, describes exact market as red-carpet prior to programme, livestreamed, rules Version/Timeframe, result N. I will not cite it as record support. Later evidence targets: full Kalshi market/rules page and resolution materials; official draw program transcript/video; red-carpet livestream metadata; Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript if accessible; Presidency Project source capture. Self-audit: strongest plaintiff proof is visible Ronaldo line in public event-linked remarks; strongest defense proof is the record's own "Prior to" label, market wording "during," and separate formal-draw archive note. Missing link: no record proof that the reporter exchange was part of the official draw program/presentation or the market-defined time period. Opening should concede the word appearance for purposes of framing, deny that event adjacency satisfies the contract, and identify what later proof would matter.

Arguments opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record scan: defendant opening filed, no new evidence before plaintiff submission. Defense concedes packet contains standalone Ronaldo but argues title 'prior to' and event boundary defeat plaintiff. Rechecked evidence list. Investigated official sources. Opened GovInfo details page DCPD-202501162: publisher Office of Federal Register/NARA, event date Dec. 5 2025, subject FIFA World Cup/JFK Center/Soccer, names include Ronaldo/Messi; note says Trump spoke at 11:13 a.m. at JFK Center, transcript released Dec. 8 and corrected Jan. 2, incomplete audio caveat. Opened GovInfo HTML transcript at https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/DCPD-202501162/html/DCPD-202501162.htm; saw relevant Ronaldo passages including 'No, but I got to meet Ronaldo...' and 'I was not Ronaldo or Messi...' Submitted faithful excerpt/metadata as ev_3fc783920414_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-3fc783920414. Adverse/formal check: opened Roll Call/Factba.se formal page https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-remarks-fifa-world-cup-drawing-december-5-2025/; page rendered; title is formal attendance/drawing remarks; browser find for Ronaldo, Cristiano, Messi returned no matches. This helps defense if formal-only boundary adopted, but our position is boundary is ambiguous and market did not clearly limit to formal staged ceremony. Plan: offer market page, transcript packet, terms, formal archive note, and GovInfo official excerpt. Include technical report/search ledger acknowledging formal-transcript adverse check. Journal updated.

Defendant merits work notes, 2026-06-29. New record scan: plaintiff added ev_3fc783920414_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-3fc783920414, GovInfo/OFR/NARA DCPD202501162 excerpt. It strengthens provenance of the pre-event reporter exchange but also preserves the title "Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw," notes remarks joined in progress, places Trump at 11:13 a.m. at the Kennedy Center, and repeats that portions could not be verified because audio was incomplete. Plaintiff argument concedes a separate formal Roll Call/Factba.se page exists and says plaintiff search found no Ronaldo there, but argues formal omission does not control the broader boundary. Defense investigation: web search for "Remarks: Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing" found Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript page and Vimeo archive page. Opened Roll Call page: title "Remarks: Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing in Washington - December 5, 2025"; page identifies speakers and full transcript. Browser rendered page find returned no matches for Ronaldo or Messi. Local Python: requests unavailable; urllib fetch of Roll Call URL returned HTTP 200, URL unchanged, HTML length 825630, string checks for Ronaldo and Messi false. HtmlParser extracted about 175864 characters; text.find('Ronaldo') and text.find('Messi') both -1. Relevant formal transcript excerpt begins with Mark Carney at 00:00:00; Infantino at 00:00:50 says "This is the World Cup trophy... we are now going to do the draw"; Trump's formal soccer answer at 00:03:14 mentions Pele/Cosmos, not Ronaldo. Submitted defense evidence accepted as ev_fbb53e87ccb3_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-fbb53e87ccb3, titled Roll Call/Factba.se formal FIFA World Cup Drawing transcript extraction. Merits theory: concede standalone Ronaldo in official pre-event reporter transcript; argue plaintiff fails event-boundary burden. Market says "during the FIFA World Cup Draw"; plaintiff's source says "Prior to"; formal transcript source states the draw is beginning and lacks the strike. Terms require words during specified time period; event-related public remarks are not automatically included absent market/source proof. Missing link: no admitted market provision or source-chain evidence showing the red-carpet/reporter exchange was included in the official Draw presentation/time period. Filing should offer market page, pre-event transcript/GovInfo excerpt, terms, formal archive note, and defense Roll Call extraction. Include technical report/search ledger with methods and absence finding.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Scanned current prompt/record: defendant submitted new evidence ev_fbb53e87ccb3_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-fbb53e87ccb3 and argued it as formal transcript omission. Stat and full read done. Evidence is 2032 bytes, Roll Call/Factba.se formal page extraction, says page title 'Remarks: Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing in Washington - December 5, 2025'; metadata lists speakers and stats; formal transcript excerpt has Infantino at 00:00:50 saying 'we are now going to do the draw'; Trump formal soccer answer at 00:03:14 discusses Pele/Cosmos; extraction/search notes say no occurrences of Ronaldo or Messi. Rebuttal strategy: accept that formal transcript omits Ronaldo, but argue it does not answer the contract boundary because there is no Kalshi market text or terms selecting that formal transcript as exclusive. Defendant's own evidence shows even formal page has pre-draw ceremonial material before 'we are now going to do the draw,' so event boundaries are not self-defining. No new external source needed; no new evidence submitted. Journal updated.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes, 2026-06-29. Scanned get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence submitted by plaintiff in rebuttal. Plaintiff rebuttal concedes Roll Call/Factba.se formal extraction contains no Ronaldo or Messi, argues that 'prior to' only gives sequence rather than market boundary, that formal page itself has preamble before mechanical draw at 00:00:50 so defendant's bright line is after-the-fact, and that terms exclude hidden categories while PX-2/PX-5 are official public records. Defense response: plaintiff bears burden of inclusion; defense does not need express exclusion if ordinary market wording and source record place the Ronaldo transcript outside the specified event. 'Prior to' is not just sequence, it is source-agency title and source class distinguishing a reporter exchange from the formal draw. Formal preamble inside the formal transcript is different from a separate reporter exchange titled prior to the event; every program has introductions, but that does not sweep in pre-program press. Official publication proves provenance but not event-period inclusion. No targeted new search needed because rebuttal raises legal interpretation of existing record and concedes the key adverse formal transcript absence. Plan: file narrow surrebuttal, offer no new evidence or technical reports unless necessary; rely on already visible exhibits and evidence ids in text.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Required evidence scan completed with list_evidence: six visible evidence items remain: four packet files (market, pre-event transcript, terms, formal-note), plaintiff GovInfo source excerpt ev_3fc783920414..., and defendant formal transcript extraction ev_fbb53e87.... No new evidence allowed in closing. Record posture: word/provenance proven by PX-2/PX-5; formal-program omission proven/accepted through defendant extraction; decisive issue is contract boundary. Closing strategy: synthesize record, concede strongest defense fact, emphasize market page lack of formal-only limitation, terms' flexible event/context language and exclusions not fitting official public transcript, and preponderance standard. Journal updated.

Defendant closing work notes, 2026-06-29. Scanned current closing prompt and record. Plaintiff closing repeats that word is proven and official; concedes formal program extraction does not contain Ronaldo/Messi; says if market said only formal drawing transcript defense would win, but no such phrase; argues GovInfo official event-linked venue remarks and terms' lack of explicit exclusion meet preponderance. Closing theory: proposition requires Yes because market and terms did not clearly exclude event-linked remarks, but the burden is plaintiff's to prove inclusion in the time period, not defendant's to produce magic words 'formal transcript only.' Decisive admitted facts: (1) market wording is 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw'; (2) Ronaldo source is officially titled 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to...' and GovInfo repeats that; (3) formal drawing transcript exists, begins the draw presentation, and has no Ronaldo/Messi; (4) terms require word during time period, so official/public provenance is separate from event-period inclusion. Strongest contrary point: market did not expressly exclude immediate pre-program public remarks and terms are flexible. Response: preponderance still asks more likely boundary; record source labels and source chain make formal draw program more likely market period than separate pre-event reporter exchange. No new evidence or technical report allowed/needed in closing.
